items.title: Robinhood rolls out public blockchain as it expands deeper into crypto
items.description: Robinhood's product push continues to blur the line between crypto and more traditional finance.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation lays out use cases for governments, institutions in new policy guide
items.description: The Ethereum Foundation said policymakers should distinguish between decentralized public blockchains and networks that remain controlled by corporations or foundations, arguing that governance structures will play a critical role in determining which platforms are suitable for long-term public sector use.

items.title: Jefferies warns against buying the dip in Circle as Open USD raises new competition fears
items.description: The investment bank said new competition from the Stripe- and Coinbase-backed stablecoin consortium could pressure USDC's growth.

items.title: Cantor says bitcoin bear market may be entering final stretch
items.description: The bank said in a note bitcoin's cycle points to a market bottom in the coming months, urging investors to focus on networks with durable value accrual.

items.title: Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400 million crypto Ponzi case
items.description: Christopher Delgado allegedly used investor funds for a lavish lifestyle, including luxury properties and vehicles, while running a fraudulent scheme from 2023 to 2026.

items.title: Bitcoin breaks above $60,000 after Fed Chair Warsh said inflation risks has come down
items.description: The Fed chair reiterated the central bank's commitment to its 2% inflation target while signaling artificial intelligence could reshape the economy and monetary policy.

items.title: EthLabs launches as Ethereum undergoes its biggest leadership transition in years
items.description: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re diving into the creation of EthLabs, and why it was launched during a period of transition for the ecosystem.

items.title: Europe's MiCA rollout sparks debate over who wins under new crypto rules
items.description: As Europe's crypto rulebook takes full effect, industry leaders agree regulation is here to stay, but disagree over whether it protects consumers or favors the biggest firms.

items.title: Bitcoin bear market ‘dead’ after first TD9 reversal signal since July 2022 fires
items.description: Bitcoin bear-market bottom signs appear as the TD9 indicator mirrors the final stages of the 2022 BTC price downtrend.

items.title: Crypto ATM bans, restrictions now in effect in Tennessee and Georgia
items.description: Crypto ATM's are fast disappearing in the US as Tennessee’s ban goes into effect today while operators in Minnesota have until Aug. 1 to comply with a similar law.

items.title: Anchorage Digital brings off-exchange settlement to Binance
items.description: The integration addresses one of the biggest obstacles keeping institutional capital on the sidelines of crypto markets: exchange counterparty risk.

items.title: Bitcoin price returns to $60K as US dollar strength rejects weekly high
items.description: Bitcoin started July with a bang, rallying above $60,000 as traders predict a relief rally as the “base case” throughout the month.

items.title: Ethereum backers launch nonprofit to lead institutional adoption efforts
items.description: Backed by Joe Lubin, BitMine and SharpLink, the independent organization will serve as a liaison for financial institutions exploring Ethereum as competition for institutional capital heats up.

items.title: French banking giant Crédit Agricole launches EURXT euro stablecoin
items.description: Crédit Agricole’s CACEIS launches a euro-pegged stablecoin on Ethereum with 20.02 million tokens issued, targeting institutional flows and tokenized fund access.

items.title: Bank of Korea governor outlines tokenized bond vision, unified ledger plan
items.description: The Bank of Korea’s governor praised tokenized government bonds for easing the issuance and management of government debt during a panel discussion at the ECB Forum.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: U.S. won’t renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
items.description: President Donald Trump's "primary" issue with USMCA centers on America's trade deficits with Canada and Mexico, a senior administration official said.

items.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
items.description: Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for free money and when the funds can be withdrawn.

items.title: Trump says outside funds 'run my money' after disclosure shows billions in 2025 revenue
items.description: The 927-page annual financial disclosure form shows Trump's purchases and sales of hundreds of companies' stocks, including Nvidia, Amazon and Microsoft.

items.title: Automakers report mixed U.S. sales results as hybrid vehicles drive market
items.description: Second-quarter U.S. new vehicle sales show a sharp divide, with automakers offering hybrid models largely outperforming those without them.

items.title: Trump administration's limits on student loan forgiveness program are blocked. What to know
items.description: Trump's policy that narrowed eligibility for a popular student loan forgiveness program will not go into effect, after two federal judges blocked the rule.

items.title: Bitcoin's summer swoon creates unique trade in Strategy
items.description: Outright shorting bitcoin or high-beta crypto equities after a steep decline carries immense tail risk.

items.title: Trump wants delay but E. Jean Carroll wants to gets paid — now: Court filing
items.description: The Supreme Court on Monday denied a request by President Donald Trump that it review a jury's verdict that he sexually abused and defamed the writer.

items.title: Lamborghini reveals new Urus performance hybrid SUV after ditching EVs
items.description: Lamborghini is calling the new Urus SE Performante the "fastest Super SUV in the world."

items.description: US president also earned hundreds of thousands of dollars from licensing of Bibles, watches and perfume

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=II3_Rum8J5M! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: CoreWeave, Nebius shares tumble as Meta stands to become a fresh threat in the cloud
items.description: Meta’s reported interest in monetizing its AI infrastructure is leading investors to question the sustainability of neocloud business models.

items.title: Is the U.S. jobs market getting better? June employment report to give us a heat check.
items.description: Is it getting easier to find a job? Are businesses really hiring more workers? That’s what the official data statistics tell us — but Americans aren’t so sure.

items.title: Protein-packed Cheerios and cat food: How General Mills plans to combat a tough spending backdrop
items.description: “Cat growth is on fire,” one executive said.

items.title: Social Security’s reality check — with a side of wishful thinking
items.description: How demographics and recent tax laws are squeezing Social Security.

items.title: Sandisk’s and Micron’s stocks sink as the rotation trade builds, but supply shortages should limit losses
items.description: Most of Sandisk’s annual revenue could eventually come from its new business model contracts that provide better visibility, BofA said.

items.title: Stock-market bulls need Kevin Warsh on their side to keep winning, says Wall Street veteran Bob Doll
items.description: The “high-risk” bull market in stocks needs Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh on its side, according to Bob Doll, CEO at Crossmark Global Investments.

items.title: Retiring abroad sounds like a dream — what could go wrong? Actually, a lot.
items.description: We hear about the older Americans who bask in apparent paradise far from home — but that’s not the whole story.

items.title: 5 charts showing how ‘dumb money’ is transforming the stock market, as retail traders smash Wall Street records
items.description: Retail trading activity set records in May and June — and the propensity of that cohort to buy the dip has looked pretty smart.

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: World Bank to phase out China lending
items.description: Decision from global development institution follows years of pressure from the Trump administration

items.title: US opts not to renew Trump’s trade deal with Mexico and Canada
items.description: Washington will instead hold annual reviews on terms of commerce with its biggest trading partners

items.title: Supreme Court checks Trump, but empowers the presidency
items.description: Despite losses on tariffs and birthright citizenship, the executive has emerged stronger

items.title: Zelenskyy urges EU to sanction Russian-owned alumina plant in Ireland
items.description: Call on the opening day of Dublin’s EU presidency adds to pressure over Aughinish Alumina smelter

items.title: Millennium and Point72 gain as hedge funds rebound from Iran war
items.description: Izzy Englander and Steve Cohen’s firms post double-digit returns in first half of year

items.title: Shares in Uber-backed Lime surge after it raised $167mn in IPO
items.description: Bike and scooter group’s shares were initially priced at $25 in deal underwritten by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and Jefferies

items.title: Saks’ retail rebirth depends more on Gucci than Wall Street
items.description: Retailer has had to repair not only the balance sheet but also the rift with luxury brands it stocked

items.title: By attacking immigrants, South Africa is destroying itself
items.description: The latest outbreak of xenophobic violence is a betrayal of the nations that aided its freedom struggle

items.title: Charles H. Townsend Dies at 82; Led Condé Nast During Digital Transition
items.description: By 2016, when he retired from the parent company of glossy magazines like Vogue, Vanity Fair and The New Yorker, print was seriously struggling.

items.title: Trump Accounts to Receive $250 Million Donation From Chip Maker Micron
items.description: President Trump said the U.S. chip maker would make a significant donation to a new type of investment account created by the administration for children.

items.title: What Would Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Prenuptial Agreement Look Like?
items.description: What would a prenuptial agreement between one of the world’s biggest pop stars and a three-time Super Bowl champion look like? Legal experts weigh in.

items.title: Fed’s Warsh Says Inflation Poses Less Risk Than a Few Weeks Ago
items.description: The Federal Reserve chairman, Kevin Warsh, stressed that the central bank would “deliver price stability,” a point he has emphasized repeatedly since taking over in May.

items.title: Anthropic Won a Reprieve From the Trump Administration. Is It Enough?
items.description: The company can restore access to its most powerful A.I. models. But Silicon Valley remains worried about the Trump administration’s heavier hand on regulation.

items.title: As Companies Race for Cheaper A.I. Options, This Start-Up Pitches a Solution
items.description: Together AI, which specializes in open-source artificial intelligence models, is now worth more than $8 billion.

items.title: What to Know About the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Trade Agreement Talks
items.description: The Trump administration said Wednesday it would not immediately request to renew the North American trade agreement, leaving businesses uncertain about the trade deal’s direction.

items.title: The South Korean Mine at the Center of America’s Tungsten Push
items.description: Deep beneath a South Korean mountain, workers are reviving a mine that could challenge China’s grip on a metal vital to weapons, chips and industry.
